---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
```


```{css}


@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Cabin:ital,wght@1,400..700&family=Roboto+Mono:ital,wght@0,100..700;1,100..700&family=Special+Gothic+Condensed+One&display=swap') ;
body{
font-family: 'Special Gothic Condensed One', sans-serif;
}
body{
background-color: #FFC7C7
 }
 body{
 font-size: 24px
 }
 
 h2 {
  background-color: #FF0000;   
  color: white;             
  display: inline-block;      
  padding: 10px 20px;          
  border-radius: 12px;        
  font-size: 40px;
  text-align: center;
}
 
 


```

## What have you (tube) been watching??

![](https://media0.giphy.com/media/v1.Y2lkPTc5MGI3NjExeTN4d2I1N2hnNmd4MzNnODJ0bjVmZnJwNXJhMGlmemI4bmx0bnJqZSZlcD12MV9naWZzX3NlYXJjaCZjdD1n/P8CC8QCewsRhRTTQpx/200.webp)


This is the observational data of you tube that I have been watching over the last fortnight. Data was collected about the time of watching, the length, retention (how much watched), along with rated scoring.

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

form_url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vSKICmRcY2_LIvnRm-IDsg0O3B3Km52MJkmtNFVyz_MRZMIc32H3AN7uJHUTQTTfyfpJFtOCE5DtJ30/pub?output=csv"

logged_data <- read_csv(form_url)
renamed_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(genre= 2, 
         start_time = 3, 
         total_length = 4,
         watched_all = 5,
         retention_time = 6, 
         video_score = 7,
         URL= 8) %>% 
         
mutate(total_length_mins = parse_number(as.character(total_length/60)))

library(tidyverse)

observations<-tibble(renamed_data) %>% 
  nrow()

total_mins<-renamed_data$total_length_mins
 max_mins <- round(max(total_mins), digits=1 )
 min_mins <- round(min(total_mins), digits=1)
 mean_mins <- round(mean(total_mins), digits=1)
 
 
  score<-renamed_data$video_score
  score_mean<-round(mean(score), digits=1)
  
  
  #this shows grouping of genre (BUT COLOURED)
bar4<-renamed_data %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = genre),
           fill = '#FF746C') +
  xlab("genres of all videos") + theme_minimal()

youtube_col = c("#800038","#9F002B","#BF0018","#DF0000","#FF1D00","#FF4B58","#FF707A","#FF959D","#FFBABF", "lightgrey", "darkgrey", "#212121")

bar5 <-renamed_data %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = watched_all, fill = genre))+
  xlab("Did I finish the youtube video?") + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  scale_fill_manual(values= youtube_col)



```

There is currently `r observations` videos in this data!

The average over all of the videos was `r mean_mins` minutes, with the shortest video being `r min_mins` minutes and the longest video being `r max_mins` minutes long.

The rating of all of videos came out to be an average of `r score_mean` out of 5 ❤️ .




```{r}
bar4
```





This graph shows what genre the video was.

```{r}
bar5

```







This graph shows if i finished the video, and what genre it was.






![](https://media1.tenor.com/m/rgiNT2-whkUAAAAd/monkey-orangutan.gif)
